lyrics:
The day was wide, the sky was mine
A single pack, a borrowed song
I swore that I would draw the line
Between the right and staying strong

The road beyond is long
Your every step will find its end
They never come around again
Yet still you ask, “How many gone?”

The years grew thick, the climb grew steep
Each dream became a kind of debt
The vows I made I could not keep
But I can’t bring myself to forget

The road is calling strong
Your every step will find its end
They never come around again
Yet still you ask, “How many gone?”

The mirror hums a quiet tune
Of faces blurred by what they’ve known
The dusk arrives too soon, too soon
And leaves me singing all alone

The road is now an evensong
Your every step will find its end
They never come around again
Yet still you ask, “How many gone?”

No answer ever stays for long
Just echoes fading, one by one
And in the hush between the dawns
You ask again, “How many gone?”
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This is an earnest and driving rock track with a clean, well-balanced production. The texture shifts effectively from sparse, clean guitar verses to dense, anthemic choruses, creating a powerful sense of emotional release and longing. The wide, clear mix allows the layers of soaring guitars and passionate vocals to build with intensity without overwhelming the listener. A distinctive quality is the song's epic dynamic range, exemplified by the powerful re-entry of the full band after a quiet, reflective bridge, which solidifies the track's journey-like feel.

review.review_text: PCBender builds 'How Many Gone?' the old-fashioned way: verses that breathe, a chorus that earns its weight, and a lyric that lands without straining for poetry. 'The mirror hums a quiet tune / Of faces blurred by what they've known' is the kind of line that stops you mid-listen, and the song's E minor pulse at a steady near-100 BPM gives it the feel of a long road actually being walked. The dynamic shift after the reflective bridge, full band crashing back in with those soaring guitars, hits the way a Springsteen album cut does when the studio finally opens up. A highlight.
anchors_used: lyric quote: 'The mirror hums a quiet tune / Of faces blurred by what they've known'
